name: Wall Slide
description: A controlled descent state that slows the character while touching a wall.
design_notes: Wall slide gives the player time to read vertical spaces and prepares wall jumps or ledge grabs. It should have a visible pose and predictable entry rules so players know when they are sliding rather than simply falling next to a wall.
design_purpose: Make wall contact readable and useful before the player commits to a wall jump.
Slow vertical failure enough to support recovery without eliminating hazards.
Create a state where level designers can place spikes, gaps, or timing challenges along walls.
player_fantasy: The player feels in control while clinging to a dangerous vertical surface.

parameters.name: slide_descent_cap
parameters.description: Maximum downward velocity while the slide is active.

parameters.name: wall_attach_angle_degrees
parameters.description: Surface-normal range considered valid for sliding.

parameters.name: slide_entry_delay_ms
parameters.description: Delay before falling contact becomes a slide state.

parameters.name: wall_stamina_drain_per_second
parameters.description: Optional resource drain while maintaining contact.

edge_cases: A player pressing into a wall at the top of a ledge should not get trapped between ledge grab and slide.
Wall slide on moving platforms must decide whether lateral movement is inherited.
Contact with decorative or one-way surfaces needs explicit slide eligibility.
Transition from wall slide to ground landing should clear wall-only friction immediately.
common_bugs: The slide state flickers because wall contact is lost for one frame on tile seams.
Downward velocity cap is applied after gravity and creates frame-rate dependent descent.
Players can slide upward when pushed by moving walls because the cap only handles falling.
Wall slide animation remains active after leaving the wall due to delayed collision callbacks.
balancing_notes: Lower descent caps make vertical routes safer; higher caps keep wall slide as a brief recovery affordance.
If wall slide feeds wall jump, tune both together so the slide does not trivialize timing.
accessibility_notes: Consider a hold-to-cling option for players who need more time to plan wall jumps.
Show valid slide surfaces through material language or consistent character pose.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an explicit movement-state enum and feed the final desired velocity into CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody movement from FixedUpdate; keep dash and jump assists outside animation callbacks. For Wall Slide, validate this path with parameters such as slide_descent_cap and wall_attach_angle_degrees.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep the mechanic state on CharacterBody2D or CharacterBody3D, update velocity in _physics_process, and separate input buffering from collision response. For Wall Slide, validate this path with parameters such as slide_descent_cap and wall_attach_angle_degrees.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement with a custom movement state or Gameplay Ability; gate activation through prediction-safe input and reconcile movement corrections cleanly in networked play. For Wall Slide, validate this path with parameters such as slide_descent_cap and wall_attach_angle_degrees.
implementation_notes.web: Represent the mechanic as deterministic state in the simulation loop, then apply resolved displacement before rendering through Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or a custom engine. For Wall Slide, validate this path with parameters such as slide_descent_cap and wall_attach_angle_degrees.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses wall contact and controlled descent as part of climbing and wall-jump traversal.

example_games.title: Super Meat Boy
example_games.note: Shows wall sliding as a readable setup state for wall jumps.
